title: sagina sabuletorum
intro: Sagina sabuletorum, also known as marsh dewgrass, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the primrose family (Primulaceae). The plant is rich in saponins and other bioactive compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Marsh dewgrass has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to utilize its health benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential interactions with other medications.
